From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

NZ rugby union club, based in Petone

Petone Rugby Club

| Club information | |
| --- | --- |
| Full name | Petone Rugby Club |
| Colours | Blue and White |
| Founded | 1885; 141 years ago (1885) |
| Website | www.petonerugby.com |
| Current details | |
| Ground | * North Park, Udy Street, Petone |
| Competition | Wellington Premiership |

The **Petone Rugby Football Club** was founded in 1885 and has been the Wellington Premier Champion 39 times between 1895 and 2005. In addition, the club has won the Club Championship on 42 occasions between 1922 and 2005. Petone is a constituent club of the [Wellington Rugby Football Union](/wiki/Wellington_Rugby_Football_Union "Wellington Rugby Football Union").

## Location

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Petone_Rugby_Club&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Location")]

The Petone Club rooms are at the lower end of the Hutt Valley on a site in Udy Street. The Clubrooms stand immediately adjacent to North Park, a field maintained to the highest standards even though it is only used for training.

Other Club facilities include:

* Outdoor training - floodlit fields at North Park and on Petone Recreation ground on the other side of Udy Street.
* A large indoor [Tiger Turf](/w/index.php?title=Tiger_Turf&action=edit&redlink=1 "Tiger Turf (page does not exist)") Stadium capable of use by a full forward pack and backline in training.
* Two large and well equipped changing and showering rooms.
* A modern and very well equipped weights room.
* A large modern fully heated lounge with full bar and kitchen facilities.
* Extensive collection of memorabilia from around the World.

## Club membership

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Petone_Rugby_Club&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Club membership")]

Club membership in 2013 totalled more than 750, made up of life members, vice presidents, honorary members, open and age grade players and 412 young players in the junior section ranging from 4 and 5 year olds in the nursery grade to 12 year olds. The club fields nine teams in the Wellington Union's 2013 competition grades from Premier to Colts U21.

The club funds a coaching coordinator, Darren 'Lardy' Larsen. Lardy is a highly qualified and competent coach. He also runs the Konnect Blue programme that allows juniors leaving for college to stay in touch with the club until they return.

## Reputation

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Petone_Rugby_Club&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Reputation")]

The club has produced 30 All Blacks and 16 NZ Maori representatives.

[Tana Umaga](/wiki/Tana_Umaga "Tana Umaga") became an All Black in 1997 and played continuously for his country before his retirement in 2006, becoming captain in 2004. [Andy Leslie](/wiki/Andy_Leslie "Andy Leslie"), a former NZ Rugby Union Vice President, was the club's first appointed All Black touring captain to Britain (1974) and South Africa (1976).

### Notable players

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Petone_Rugby_Club&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Notable players")]

* [Allan Hewson](/wiki/Allan_Hewson "Allan Hewson") – [All Black](/wiki/All_Blacks "All Blacks")
* [Neemia Tialata](/wiki/Neemia_Tialata "Neemia Tialata") – All Black
* [Tana Umaga](/wiki/Tana_Umaga "Tana Umaga") – All Black captain
* [Andy Leslie](/wiki/Andy_Leslie "Andy Leslie") – All Black captain
* [Ken Gray](/wiki/Ken_Gray_(All_Black) "Ken Gray (All Black)") – All Black
* [Hercules Richard Wright](/wiki/Hercules_Richard_Wright "Hercules Richard Wright") – [Kiwi](/wiki/New_Zealand_national_rugby_league_team "New Zealand national rugby league team")
* [Daniel Fraser](/wiki/Daniel_Fraser_(rugby_league) "Daniel Fraser (rugby league)") – Kiwi
* [Edward Tyne](/wiki/Edward_Tyne "Edward Tyne") – Kiwi
* [Tom Cross](/wiki/Tom_Cross_(rugby) "Tom Cross (rugby)") – All Black & Kiwi
* [Arthur Kelly](/wiki/Arthur_Kelly_(rugby_league) "Arthur Kelly (rugby league)") – Kiwi
* [Duncan McGregor](/wiki/Duncan_McGregor "Duncan McGregor") – All Black & Kiwi
* [Riki Flutey](/wiki/Riki_Flutey "Riki Flutey") – England
* [Mark Nicholls](/wiki/Mark_Nicholls_(rugby_union) "Mark Nicholls (rugby union)") – All Black
* [Horace Nunn](/wiki/Horace_Nunn "Horace Nunn") - Kiwi

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Petone_Rugby_Club&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: History")]

[Petone](/wiki/Petone "Petone") had a four-year reign as Premier Champions between 1904 and 1907 and the Club set a memorable record in the 1907 season when it won all championship grades in the Wellington competition. After the 1907 season the club was weakened by inadvertently helping the formation of [rugby league](/wiki/Rugby_league "Rugby league") in New Zealand. The club provided six players for the professional [1907–1908 rugby tour of Great Britain](/wiki/1907%E2%80%9308_New_Zealand_rugby_tour_of_Australia_and_Great_Britain "1907–08 New Zealand rugby tour of Australia and Great Britain") including its two [All Blacks](/wiki/All_Blacks "All Blacks") at the time, [Tom Cross](/wiki/Tom_Cross_(rugby) "Tom Cross (rugby)") and [Duncan McGregor](/wiki/Duncan_McGregor "Duncan McGregor"), and the club's captain, [Hercules Wright](/wiki/Hercules_Wright "Hercules Wright").[[1]](#cite_note-1)
The [New Zealand Rugby Union](/wiki/New_Zealand_Rugby_Union "New Zealand Rugby Union") subsequently banned all members of the tour for life.

In the 1971 season the Premier team won the Premier competition and [the Jubilee Cup](/wiki/The_Jubilee_Cup "The Jubilee Cup") for the fifth consecutive season and became the first club in the history of Wellington rugby to achieve this feat since the club competition was commenced in 1868. The first three wins in this sequence were achieved under the leadership of [Ken Gray](/wiki/Ken_Gray_(All_Black) "Ken Gray (All Black)"), widely considered one of the greatest All Black prop forwards ever. The Ken Gray Academy was established in 1995 in Ken's name to assist in the development of the best young players wishing to find their way through the Club to the highest representative honours.

The centennial history records that [Te Puni](/wiki/H%C5%8Dniana_Te_Puni-k%C5%8Dkopu "Hōniana Te Puni-kōkopu") arrived in the Hutt Valley in 1817 and died there in 1870. In the first official match ever played by the Petone Club, three descendants of Te Puni were in the fifteen. Today Petone Rugby is a multi-cultural mix in a family-oriented environment.

In November 2006, the club hosted [The World Golden Oldies Festival](/w/index.php?title=The_World_Golden_Oldies_Festival&action=edit&redlink=1 "The World Golden Oldies Festival (page does not exist)") and in 2010 the club celebrated its 125th anniversary.

### Monogram

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Petone_Rugby_Club&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Monogram")]

The club adopted its familiar monogram on the recommendation of its illustrious Life Member and Maori All Black Matt Love as part of its 50th jubilee celebrations in 1935. The monogram is embellished by the words in Maori "kia kaha kia maia" – broadly translated as "give of your best – be confident in your own ability". This reflects the strength of the partnership with Maori in the Club.
The monogram was updated in 2010 to include "Est 1885" and the words "Petone Rugby Club".
